response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced software developer with a broad background across web, mobile, and some embedded work, but he fails to meet several hard requirements for this role. Most critically, there is no mention of Japanese language proficiency anywhere in the resume, which is listed as a non-negotiable requirement. His technical profile is web/backend-oriented (TypeScript, Node.js, Ember.js, DLang) with no evident experience in embedded networking security, LAN-switch firmware, MACsec, 802.1X, network switch architectures, or Linux kernel security work. The C/C++ experience is minimal and dated (National Instruments, 2014–2015; a bachelor thesis project). This role requires deep, current expertise in embedded C, networking security protocols, and Japanese fluency — none of which this candidate demonstrates.
strengths: 15+ years of software development experience across diverse environments, demonstrating broad technical adaptability
Some C and C++ exposure (National Instruments IDE components, embedded sound synthesis thesis), indicating non-zero systems programming background
Linux experience evident throughout career; fluent English for reading technical documentation and email communication
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency mentioned — this is a hard, non-negotiable requirement that alone disqualifies the candidate
Zero demonstrable experience with security protocols relevant to the role (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption key exchange, tamper detection)
No embedded firmware, network switch, or Layer-2/Layer-3 networking experience; career is overwhelmingly web and SaaS-focused
